questions.question: Two gas jars are set up as shown. beforeafter jar 2 jar 1 jar 2 jar 1 lid air brown gas The lid is removed and the gas jars are left to stand. After some time the contents of both gas jars are brown. Which process causes this to happen? A condensation, B diffusion, C evaporation, D filtration

After some time the contents of both gas jars are brown. Which process causes this to happen? A condensation, B diffusion, C evaporation, D filtration

questions.text: Which piece of apparatus is used to measure variable quantities of liquid in a titration? ABCD

questions.question: Element Q has 4 electrons in its outer shell and has 69 neutrons. Q conducts electricity. What is Q? A carbon (C), B lead (Pb), C thulium (Tm), D tin (Sn)

questions.question: Which statement describes positive ions? A Positive ions have more electrons than neutrons., B Positive ions have more protons than neutrons., C Positive ions have more electrons than protons., D Positive ions have more protons than electrons.
questions.text: Which statement describes positive ions? A Positive ions have more electrons than neutrons., B Positive ions have more protons than neutrons., C Positive ions have more electrons than protons., D Positive ions have more protons than electrons.
options: A Positive ions have more electrons than neutrons.
B Positive ions have more protons than neutrons.
C Positive ions have more electrons than protons.
D Positive ions have more protons than electrons.

questions.question: The electronic structures of atoms X and Y are shown. XY X and Y form a covalent compound. What is its formula? A X 2 Y B XY C XY 2, D XY 6
questions.text: The electronic structures of atoms X and Y are shown. XY X and Y form a covalent compound. What is its formula? A X 2 Y B XY C XY 2, D XY 6
options: A X 2 Y B XY C XY 2

questions.question: Which statement about reactions that produce heat is not correct? A Burning magnesium produces heat energy., B The overall reaction is exothermic., C The products have more energy than the reactants., D The temperature of the surroundings increases.
questions.text: Which statement about reactions that produce heat is not correct? A Burning magnesium produces heat energy., B The overall reaction is exothermic., C The products have more energy than the reactants., D The temperature of the surroundings increases.
options: A Burning magnesium produces heat energy.
B The overall reaction is exothermic.
C The products have more energy than the reactants.
D The temperature of the surroundings increases.

questions.question: Which changes increase the rate of reaction? 1   increasing the concentration of the reactants 2   increasing the particle size of a solid reactant 3   increasing the temperature A 1, 2 and 3 B 1 and 2 only C 1 and 3 only D 2 and 3 only
questions.text: Which changes increase the rate of reaction? 1   increasing the concentration of the reactants 2   increasing the particle size of a solid reactant 3   increasing the temperature A 1, 2 and 3 B 1 and 2 only C 1 and 3 only D 2 and 3 only
options: A 1, 2 and 3 B 1 and 2 only C 1 and 3 only D 2 and 3 only

questions.question: Iron is extracted from iron oxide using carbon monoxide as shown. iron oxide  +  carbon monoxide  →  iron  +  carbon dioxide Which statement is correct? A Carbon monoxide is oxidised to carbon dioxide., B Carbon monoxide is reduced to carbon dioxide., C Iron is oxidised to iron oxide., D Iron oxide is oxidised to iron.
questions.text: Iron is extracted from iron oxide using carbon monoxide as shown. iron oxide  +  carbon monoxide  →  iron  +  carbon dioxide Which statement is correct? A Carbon monoxide is oxidised to carbon dioxide., B Carbon monoxide is reduced to carbon dioxide., C Iron is oxidised to iron oxide., D Iron oxide is oxidised to iron.
options: A Carbon monoxide is oxidised to carbon dioxide.
B Carbon monoxide is reduced to carbon dioxide.
C Iron is oxidised to iron oxide.
D Iron oxide is oxidised to iron.

questions.question: Four different solutions, W, X, Y and Z, are tested with Universal Indicator. solution          W          X          Y          Z colour with Universal Indicator green      red      purple      orange Which solutions are acidic? A W and Z B X and Z C X only D Y only
questions.text: Four different solutions, W, X, Y and Z, are tested with Universal Indicator. solution          W          X          Y          Z colour with Universal Indicator green      red      purple      orange Which solutions are acidic? A W and Z B X and Z C X only D Y only
options: A W and Z B X and Z C X only D Y only

questions.question: In  the  Periodic  Table,  how  does  the  metallic  character  of  the  elements  vary  from  left  to  right across a period? A It   decreases., B It   increases., C It increases then decreases., D It stays the same.

questions.question: The elements in a group of the Periodic Table show the following trends. 1   The element with the lowest proton number has the lowest reactivity. 2   All the elements in the group form basic oxides. 3   The density of the elements increases down the group. 4   The melting point of the elements decreases down the group. In which group are the elements found? A I   B   IV   C   VI   D   VII 8
questions.text: The elements in a group of the Periodic Table show the following trends. 1   The element with the lowest proton number has the lowest reactivity. 2   All the elements in the group form basic oxides. 3   The density of the elements increases down the group. 4   The melting point of the elements decreases down the group. In which group are the elements found? A I   B   IV   C   VI   D   VII 8
options: A I   B   IV   C   VI   D   VII 8

questions.question: Uranium is a radioactive element but it is also a typical metal. What is not a property of uranium? A It can be hammered into shape., B It conducts heat., C It is used as a source of energy., D It forms covalent compounds.
questions.text: Uranium is a radioactive element but it is also a typical metal. What is not a property of uranium? A It can be hammered into shape., B It conducts heat., C It is used as a source of energy., D It forms covalent compounds.
options: A It can be hammered into shape.
C It is used as a source of energy.

B ammonium nitrate and potassium sulfate
C potassium nitrate and calcium phosphate
D potassium nitrate and potassium sulfate

A Hydrocarbon W has the formula CH 4 and can be polymerised., B Hydrocarbon X has the formula C 2 H 4 and can be polymerised., C Hydrocarbon Y has the formula C 3 H 6 and can be polymerised., D Hydrocarbon Z has the formula C 4 H 10 and can be polymerised. 13

options: A Hydrocarbon W has the formula CH 4 and can be polymerised.
B Hydrocarbon X has the formula C 2 H 4 and can be polymerised.
C Hydrocarbon Y has the formula C 3 H 6 and can be polymerised.
D Hydrocarbon Z has the formula C 4 H 10 and can be polymerised. 13
